name: Resizable Navbar
summary: A scroll-aware navigation bar that shrinks width and adds backdrop blur when scrolling down the page.
detailedDescription: Resizable Navbar creates an intelligent navigation system that responds to scroll position. The Navbar component tracks scrollY using Framer Motion's useScroll hook with 'start start' to 'end start' offset. When scroll exceeds 100px, visible state triggers NavBody transformation: width shrinks from 100% to 40%, backdrop-filter applies blur(10px), boxShadow adds multi-layer depth, and y translates 20px down. Spring animation (stiffness: 200, damping: 50) smooths transitions. The NavBody has 800px minWidth constraint, rounded-full styling, and hidden/flex breakpoints (hidden mobile, flex lg+). Background transitions from transparent to white/80 (dark: neutral-950/80). The system uses React.Children.map to clone children with visible prop, enabling coordinated child animations. Mobile nav support via IconMenu2/IconX toggles. NavItems render horizontal link lists with hover states. This creates adaptive navigation that maximizes screen real estate at top, then condenses to compact floating bar on scroll - ideal for long-form content, documentation, or landing pages where navigation should be prominent initially then recede.
recommendations: Good fit for dashboards, admin panels, and data-dense application layouts.
Suitable for performance-sensitive pages that still need polished visuals.
Combine with hero and feature sections to establish clear top-level page flow.
